title: Introducing World Of Taxonomy
description: A unified knowledge graph connecting 1,000+ classification systems, 1.2M+ codes, and 321K+ crosswalk edges. The open-source Rosetta Stone for global taxonomies.
author: Ram Katamaraja

title: Why Classification Systems Are Broken
description: Every country and industry body has its own coding system. Translating between them costs hours, teams, and millions. Here is why the fragmentation exists and what we can do about it.
author: Ram Katamaraja

title: 1,000 Systems, 1.2M+ Nodes, 321K Crosswalk Edges: How We Built the Knowledge Graph
description: The architecture and ingestion pipeline behind World Of Taxonomy - from authoritative source files to a unified queryable graph of global classification systems.
author: Ram Katamaraja

title: Translating NAICS to NACE in One API Call
description: A practical walkthrough for data engineers who need to reconcile North American industry codes with EU classification systems using the World Of Taxonomy API.
author: Ram Katamaraja

title: Give Your AI Agent Access to Every Taxonomy
description: Set up the World Of Taxonomy MCP server with Claude, Cursor, VS Code, Windsurf, or any MCP-compatible client in under 5 minutes.
author: Ram Katamaraja

title: Medical Coding Made Navigable
description: ICD-10-CM, ICD-11, LOINC, SNOMED CT, and NCI Thesaurus in one searchable graph. How World Of Taxonomy helps health IT teams navigate clinical classification systems.
author: Ram Katamaraja

title: Trade Classification for Global Commerce
description: HS, UNSPSC, CPC, SITC, HTS, and Schedule B - how World Of Taxonomy connects the trade classification systems that power global customs and compliance.
author: Ram Katamaraja

title: Mapping Occupations Across Borders
description: SOC, ISCO-08, ESCO, O*NET, ANZSCO, NOC - how to translate job classifications between countries for HR, recruitment, and labor market analysis.
author: Ram Katamaraja

title: Self-Host World Of Taxonomy in 2 Minutes
description: Clone the repo, run docker compose up, and you have the full knowledge graph running locally. A step-by-step guide to self-hosting World Of Taxonomy.
author: Ram Katamaraja

title: Contributing a New Classification System
description: A TDD-enforced guide to adding a classification system to World Of Taxonomy. Three paths: NACE-derived, ISIC-derived, or standalone ingester.
author: Ram Katamaraja

title: The Crosswalk Graph: Visualizing How Systems Connect
description: How the interactive crosswalk ring visualization reveals the hidden connections between 1,000 classification systems and what the edges actually mean.
author: Ram Katamaraja

title: 249 Countries, One Taxonomy Profile Each
description: Every country has official, regional, and recommended classification systems. World Of Taxonomy profiles all 249 so you always know which codes apply where.
author: Ram Katamaraja

title: Building a REST API for 1.2 Million Codes
description: FastAPI, asyncpg, and PostgreSQL full-text search - the technical decisions behind serving 1.2 million classification codes with sub-100ms response times.
author: Ram Katamaraja

title: From NAICS to NIC: How National Industry Codes Diverge
description: The US uses NAICS, India uses NIC, the EU uses NACE, Japan uses JSIC. A deep comparison of how different countries slice the same economic activity.
author: Ram Katamaraja

title: Life Sciences Classification: Navigating Pharma, Clinical, and Biotech Taxonomies
description: ATC, NDC, ICD, LOINC, NCI Thesaurus, GMDN, FDA regulations - how World Of Taxonomy connects the fragmented classification landscape across pharmaceuticals, clinical coding, biotech, and medical devices.
author: Ram Katamaraja
